title: Caching Required: High Write Throughput
description: Policy pattern that enforces caching when write QPS reaches 500+ req/s — the threshold at which synchronous database writes become a throughput bottleneck and write batching or buffering is required.

cost.costNotes: Meta-policy pattern with no direct cost. Actual write caching infrastructure costs are accounted for in the write cache strategy pattern selected downstream (write-through-cache, write-back-cache).

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Zero cost — meta-policy pattern only. Write caching infrastructure costs are in write-through-cache and write-back-cache patterns.
provenance.adoptionCost: Zero adoption cost — this pattern auto-detects write QPS requirements from the spec and enforces caching as a hard compilation requirement.
provenance.licenseCost: No license cost — policy enforcement pattern only.

provides.reasoning: Detects when write throughput exceeds the threshold where direct database writes become a bottleneck, and enforces /constraints/features/caching: true as a hard compilation gate so a write cache strategy is selected.

requires.reasoning: At 500+ write QPS, a write cache layer (write-through or write-back) is required to absorb burst write load or reduce write latency. The specific implementation is determined by the downstream cache strategy pattern.

availability.reason: General architectural enforcement pattern applicable to any high-write-throughput system

supports_nfr.reason: Pattern activates when write QPS reaches 500+ req/s — the threshold at which synchronous database writes typically become a latency or throughput bottleneck for standard managed database instances.
Pattern is compatible with any authentication mechanism supported by the underlying cache infrastructure.
Pattern is compatible with both audited and non-audited environments; audit requirements are enforced at the cache strategy level.
Write caching supports all tenant isolation strategies through tenant-scoped cache key namespacing.
Pattern is compatible with both PII and non-PII workloads; PII requires cache encryption and appropriate flush policies.
Compatible with GDPR when write cache flush and invalidation cover right-to-be-forgotten requirements.
Compatible with HIPAA when cache stores use encryption at rest and in transit.
Compatible with CCPA when cache flush handles data deletion requests before persistence.
SOX workloads requiring strict audit trails should prefer write-through (synchronous persistence) over write-back (async flush with data loss risk).
Consistency enforcement is delegated to the downstream write cache strategy pattern.
Durability enforcement is delegated to the downstream write cache strategy pattern (write-back requires durability/strict: false; write-through supports either).

supports_constraints.reason: Pattern is cloud-agnostic; write caching infrastructure exists on all major clouds and on-premises.
Pattern is language-agnostic; write cache client libraries exist for all major languages.
High write QPS can occur on any platform type; write caching enforcement is platform-agnostic.
Write caching is compatible with both async and sync messaging architectures.
High write QPS can occur in AI inference pipelines (embedding writes, result logging) but pattern is not AI-specific.
Write caching supports both single-tenant and multi-tenant architectures.
High write QPS occurs in both real-time and batch write workloads.
Meta-pattern activates regardless; distributed_transactions compatibility is enforced by the downstream write cache strategy (write-back is incompatible, write-through is compatible).
High write QPS is common in real-time streaming ingestion pipelines.
Write caching can buffer vector embedding writes before persistence.
Write caching works with document stores as the backing database.
Write caching works with key-value stores as the backing database.
Write caching can buffer graph mutation writes before persistence.
Write caching is a natural fit for high-write-QPS time-series ingestion pipelines.
Meta-pattern activates regardless; OLTP compatibility is enforced by the downstream write cache strategy (write-back is incompatible with OLTP, write-through is compatible).
Write caching can buffer analytical result writes before persistence.

requires_constraints.reason: At 500+ write QPS, a write caching strategy is mandatory to prevent database write saturation or unacceptable write latency. Set constraints.features.caching: true

requires_nfr.reason: Write caching improves availability under write burst load, but the cache+DB stack is bounded at four nines without additional redundancy.
Write-through adds synchronous dual-write overhead; write-back adds cache-write latency. Floor is 1ms for in-memory cache writes.
Tail latency from cache contention, flush coordination, or backing store pressure under high write load.
Write cache recovery requires flushing pending writes or replaying state; minimum RTO is 1 minute.
Write-through guarantees RPO=0; write-back may lose in-flight writes (RPO > 0). Downstream pattern enforces the specific RPO constraint.
Write caching infrastructure supports up to 1M writes/sec (3.6B/hour) with a properly sized cache cluster.

warn_nfr.reason: At 500+ write QPS with strict durability, write-through is required (write-back is incompatible). Write-through incurs dual-write latency overhead; consider if strict durability is truly necessary at this scale.
warn_nfr.message: caching-required-high-write-throughput: spec requires durability/strict=true at {actual} write QPS. Write-through will be enforced (write-back incompatible), adding synchronous dual-write latency. Verify strict durability is required at this scale.

warn_nfr.reason: Pattern activates at exactly 500 write QPS. If actual QPS is only slightly above threshold (500-600), consider if architectural overhead of write caching justifies the complexity vs. scaling the database instance.
warn_nfr.message: caching-required-high-write-throughput: write QPS is {actual} req/s, just above the 500 req/s caching threshold. Consider if database instance scaling is simpler than introducing write cache complexity.
